name: Salmon en Papillote
tagline: Salmon and vegetables steamed in parchment with herbs and lemon
highlight: Salmon plus veg in a sealed parchment — silky and clean

hero.alt: Salmon en papillote opened to reveal salmon and vegetables
hero.caption: papillote — steamed in parchment

healthFacts: Steaming in parchment locks in moisture and aromatics — no fat needed.
Vegetables released their juices to steam-poach the salmon.

steps.title: Cut parchment hearts
steps.detail: Fold parchment in half; cut a large heart shape — when opened it'll be a circle. Two hearts for two portions.
steps.tip: Heart shape gives you a long crimping edge — fewer leaks.

steps.title: Layer the bed
steps.detail: On one half of each heart, layer zucchini, tomatoes, shallot, garlic. Place salmon on top, salt, pepper. Top with lemon, thyme, drizzle olive oil and wine.
steps.tip: Veg under fish acts as both flavor and rack.

steps.title: Crimp into a packet
steps.detail: Fold the empty half over. Crimp the edges by folding small pleats all around to seal — must be airtight.
steps.tip: Brush edges with water before crimping for a tighter seal.

steps.title: Bake and serve
steps.detail: Place packets on a sheet pan. Bake at 400°F for 12 min — they'll puff. Bring packets to the table; let diners cut them open.
steps.tip: The puffed packet release of steam is half the joy. Theatre.
